events.description: v0.3.4 (additive, W9): broadcast-event payloads declared with `event Name { … }`. Mirrors `records` in shape but lives in its own bucket so generators can fan out pub/sub emitters separately from state.

transport.description: v0.3.4 (additive, W8): schema-level intended transport projected from `@transport(kind: ...)` (SPEC §7.14). Absent ≡ 'any'. Consulted by generators for E025 mismatch errors. Pre-0.3.4 consumers ignore.

directives.description: v0.3.4 (additive, W8): schema-level directives in source order. `@transport` is also projected into `transport`; this array preserves the raw list for uniform walks.

leadingComments.description: v0.3.2 (additive): raw `#`-comment lines immediately preceding the declaration (leading `#` + one optional space stripped). Absent when no leading comments were attached (not `[]`). No built-in semantics — generator extension point.

map.description: v0.3: true when field is Map<K,V>. `type` is then the literal string 'Map'.

mapKey.description: v0.3: key type; present iff map=true. v0.3.4: `mapKey.required` is always `true` — see SPEC §4.8 R023 (map keys are never null). Syntactic `!` on the key is redundant; parsers normalise both `Map<K, V>` and `Map<K!, V>` to the same IR.

mapValue.description: v0.3: value type; present iff map=true. `mapValue.required` reflects the syntactic `!` on V (SPEC §4.3).

TypeRef.description: v0.3: nested type reference used inside map key/value slots. Recursively describes scalars, lists, and nested maps.

outputList.description: v0.3.1: true when output is a list [T] / [T!] / [T]! / [T!]!. `output` still carries the element's base type name.

outputListItemRequired.description: v0.3.1: meaningful only when outputList=true. True iff the list element carries `!`.

Event.description: v0.3.4 (additive, W9): broadcast-event payload declaration. Shape mirrors `Record` exactly — same fields, same directives, same leading-comments passthrough — but with pub/sub (not state) semantics. See SPEC §5.5.

argTypes.description: v0.3.3 (additive): per-argument literal-kind tag. Keys mirror `args` exactly. Values are one of 'string' | 'int' | 'float' | 'bool' | 'enum_ref' | 'list'. Present iff the directive has at least one argument. Generators MUST consult `argTypes[k] === 'enum_ref'` to distinguish a bare-identifier enum literal (per SPEC R041) from a string literal when emitting defaults; pre-0.3.3 consumers may ignore and fall back to resolving by enclosing field type.
